titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 76749 (previously, BM Loans, 29/355)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=332 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:17:14.913077+00 | translation moa=333 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:17:16.885967+00

div.p:
Robertus de londoniis omnibus hominibus suis tam futuris quam presentibus clericis ac laicis . francis et anglicis . salutem . No
tum sit omnibus tam futuris quam presentibus quod ego Robertus de londoniis dedi et concessi . et hac mea carta confirmaui Waltero
de berkelaie cognato meo unam carrucatam terre in lassedwine . Et plainmalaur mea terram de tindale pro hominio et ser
uicio suo . in feudo et hereditate . sibi et heredibus suis de me et heredibus meis tenendam . libere et quiete . plenarie et honorifice . in
bosco et in plano . in pratis et pascuis . in aquis et molendinis . reddendo mihi et heredibus meis annuatim duos ancipitres soros pro
seruicio quod ad me pertinet . saluo seruicio Regis . his testibus . Willelmo filio odardi . et Willelmo de foscote . et Willelmo de Cvningburc .
et mauricio de Reigni . et Henrico filio iordani . et Willelmo clerico de chausi . et Gregorio persona . et Rogero clerico qui hanc cartam
dictatuit . et Ricardo de cameis . et Willelmo de thoeni . et Radulfo beleth . et Macus prepostio . et Roberto bochart . et hosmundo
famulo . et Waltero filio Gaufridi . et Paruo consilio . et Vnwine .

p.cl: Robert of London, to all his own men, future as well as present, clerk and lay, French and English, greeting.

Be it known to all, future as well as present, that I, Robert of London, gave, conceded, and by this my charter made firm, to Walter of Berkeley my cousin, one ploughgate of land in Lessudden; and Plenmeller, my land of Tynedale; in return for his homage and service, in feu and heritage; for him and his heirs to hold of me and my heirs, free and quit, fully and honourably, in wood and in plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and mills, rendering to me and my heirs annually two sore sparrowhawks by way of the service which pertains to me, reserving the king's service.

With these as witnesses: William son of Odard; William of Forscote; William of Conisbrough; Maurice de Reingny; Henry son of Jordan; William the clerk of Chausi; Gregory the parson; Roger the clerk, who composed this charter; Richard de Cameis; William de Tosny; Ralph Beleth; Maccus the provost; Robert Bochart; Osmund the servant; Walter son of Geoffrey; 'Paruum consilium' [i.e. 'Modest advice']; and Unwine.
